source_jobs.title: Customer Success Manager
source_jobs.description:
Arcadia is dedicated to happier, healthier days for all. We believe that there is a better healthcare world – one powered by data. Our platform transforms complex, diverse data into a unified foundation for health, helping organizations deliver better care, boost revenue, and lower costs. 
 
We’re a team of fiercely driven individuals committed to making healthcare more sustainable—and we’re looking for passionate people to help us get there.
 
For more information, visit arcadia.io.
 
Why This Role Is Important to Arcadia 
 
At Arcadia.io, the Customer Success Manager (CSM) has complete responsibility for end-to-end customer success for Arcadia’s non-enterprise clients. CSMs commit to the ongoing work of making satisfied customers, through ensuring collective adoption of Arcadia capabilities, strong data quality, rapid and effective support, and smooth upgrades. The CSM displays an intimate understanding of Arcadia functionality and can marry this understanding with their knowledge of value-based care to lead product training, mitigate issues, and reconfigure tools to meet new business goals. A CSM’s experience and skills enable them to independently manage a portfolio of customer accounts operating as the clients’ primary point of contact. CSMs build long term relationships with their clients and are vested in their success.
 
CSMs have a knack for connecting strategic business objectives to Arcadia’s capabilities, enjoy getting into the weeds, and excel at critical problem solving. The CSM leads cross-functional teams to effectively troubleshoot, and project manage complex technical issues. As the customer’s primary point of contact, the CSM will prioritize executing projects to support each client’s business objectives, increasing the adoption of Arcadia tools and capabilities, manage post-initial implementation projects, communicate timelines, and be the first line of expertise and support for Arcadia customers.
 
In this position, you will work closely with division leaders, implementation management, production support SMEs, engineering, product managers and service desk teams.
 
What Success Looks Like
In 3 months
·  Complete Arcadia Core Platform certification and onboarding requirements
·  Successfully onboard to all customers in assigned portfolio 
·  Coordinate with internal teams to create and execute against project timelines
·  Communicate project status updates to customer leadership
·  Provide support to assigned customer accounts with oversight
·  Know each assigned customers’ scope of work and overarching Arcadia use cases
·  Drive issue resolution through coordination with internal teams
·  Show a base understanding of how Arcadia supports several population health programs focused on Cost, Utilization, Quality and Risk performance 
·  Have a strong understanding of Arcadia’s security protocols and processes
·  Convey ideas and solutions effectively, professionally and with the appropriate sensitivity through verbal and written communication  
 
In 6 months
·  Act as the primary CSM on assigned customer accounts with minimal oversight
·  Manage day to day operations of each customer
·  Train customers on the base platform and be able to tailor the training to customers’ needs
·  Understand Arcadia’s data ingestion processes and be able to troubleshoot processing issues at a high-level
·  Be knowledgeable on a customer’s strategic goals and initiatives supported through Arcadia
·  Be able to guide clients through recommended product configurations that are in alignment with clients’ goals and initiatives 
·  Assist in leading and executing planning and adoption workshops with customers  
·  Use technical skills and critical thinking to assist in investigating highly complex issues. Develop and execute against remediation plans
·  Understand and translate issue impact relative to customers’ business needs
·  Full accountability for managing small to medium implementation projects for Arcadia data feeds and ap

source_jobs.title: Director, Analytics
source_jobs.description:
Arcadia is dedicated to happier, healthier days for all. We believe that there is a better healthcare world – one powered by data. Our platform transforms complex, diverse data into a unified foundation for health, helping organizations deliver better care, boost revenue, and lower costs.
 
We’re a team of fiercely driven individuals committed to making healthcare more sustainable—and we’re looking for passionate people to help us get there.
 
For more information, visit arcadia.io.
 
 
Why This Role Is Important to Arcadia 

The Director, Analytics is a senior level position responsible for shaping analytic strategy and delivering high-impact insights across complex customer engagements. This role reports directly to the VP of Analytics and operates as the highest-level analytic authority on our productionalized analytics engines and dashboards; driving methodological rigor, influencing business strategy, and ensuring analytics directly support contractual commitments and measurable performance outcomes.

This individual will lead a team of SQL analysts and BI developers to deliver scalable analytic solutions. Given the scale, visibility, and complexity of the work, this role requires deep healthcare domain expertise, advanced technical capability, strong executive presence, and the ability to operate autonomously in ambiguous environments.

 
What Success Looks Like
In 3 months
  • Develop deep understanding of enterprise data architecture, reporting commitments, and performance objectives
  • Establish credibility as a strategic analytic partner to senior leaders and to your direct reports
  • Contribute to builds across backend and frontend deliverables
  • Provide guidance on complex analytic workstreams and influence prioritization decisions
  • Build trust from team of 4-8 analysts/dashboarders and understand how team fits into priorities
  • Work to refine current OKRs and build team out necessary to execute on those OKRs
In 6 months
  • Drive measurable improvements in analytic quality, scalability, and efficiency
  • Lead cross-functional initiatives requiring advanced methodological expertise
  • Serve as trusted advisor on medical economics, risk, episodes, and dashboarding
  • Create rigorous performance management framework for team to be evaluated against
  • Ensure AI integration into analytics workflows
In 12 months 
  • Be recognized as an enterprise subject matter expert across analytics, medical economics, risk, episodes and dashboarding
  • Influence strategic decisions through data-backed recommendations
  • Demonstrate measurable impact on customer performance outcomes and business growth
  • Build durable analytic infrastructure and frameworks that scale across engagements

source_jobs.title: Director, Training and Enablement
source_jobs.description:
Arcadia is dedicated to happier, healthier days for all. We believe that there is a better healthcare world – one powered by data. Our platform transforms complex, diverse data into a unified foundation for health, helping organizations deliver better care, boost revenue, and lower costs. 
 
We’re a team of fiercely driven individuals committed to making healthcare more sustainable—and we’re looking for passionate people to help us get there.
 
For more information, visit arcadia.io.
 
 
Why This Role Is Important to Arcadia
The Director of Training & Enablement is responsible for shaping how customers, partners, and employees learn, adopt, and drive outcome improvement through the Arcadia platform. This role operates as the highest-level authority for product and domain training strategy and education at Arcadia, driving programmatic rigor, influencing go-to-market strategy impacting both sales and existing customer experience, and ensuring enablement directly supports customer onboarding outcomes, product adoption, and measurable performance results. 
This individual will lead Arcadia’s most complex training initiatives including new product education, serve as a strategic partner to executive stakeholders across Product, Customer Success, Growth and clinical leadership, and elevate enablement standards across the organization. Given the breadth of the role — spanning new customer onboarding, asynchronous learning infrastructure, product release readiness, internal team enablement, and certification programs — this position requires deep health IT domain expertise including Value-Based Care knowledge, instructional design acumen, strong operational discipline, and the ability to build and lead in a high-growth environment. 
Reporting directly to the Chief Medical Officer, this role will lead education and be responsible for customer, partner and employee product competency. Healthcare technology is at an inflection point, and this role will be challenged to ensure that Arcadia’s customers are brought forward into work that leverages interoperability and automation. 
Training and Enablement (Medical Economics expert), you will leverage your extensive, operational, experience in medical economics to tangibly shape some of our newest customer programs.  Your expertise in value-based care, medical economics, including risk adjustment, cost management, network optimization, contract forecasting, will directly impact the effectiveness of our training programs, and customer adoption strategies.
 
What Success Looks Like
In 3 months
Develop a deep understanding of the Arcadia platform, product roadmap, and existing training assets and gaps
Establish relationships and credibility with key stakeholders across Product, Growth, Customer Success and Implementation
Complete an audit of current customer onboarding and training programs, identify product training gaps, inventory educational delivery modes and deliver a prioritized enablement roadmap
Define KPI’s for the Learning and Enablement area
Begin embedding into the product release cycle to deliver training readiness
 In 6 months

source_jobs.title: Enterprise Project Associate
source_jobs.description:
Arcadia is dedicated to happier, healthier days for all. We believe that there is a better healthcare world – one powered by data. Our platform transforms complex, diverse data into a unified foundation for health, helping organizations deliver better care, boost revenue, and lower costs. 

We’re a team of fiercely driven individuals committed to making healthcare more sustainable—and we’re looking for passionate people to help us get there.

For more information, visit arcadia.io.

Why This Role Is Important to Arcadia

The Enterprise Project Associate is a foundational role within Arcadia's Enterprise Customer Partnerships team, designed as the first step toward becoming a full Enterprise Implementation Manager (EIM). This position offers immediate client exposure and autonomous task ownership from day one.

You will work directly with healthcare organizations navigating value-based care transformation, supporting implementations that drive measurable business impact. This role blends analytical rigor with consultative partnership—you're not just configuring software, you're helping customers achieve outcomes.

What Success Looks Like
In 3 months
- Build working knowledge of Arcadia’s platform, data workflows, and implementation playbooks
- Own scoped tasks and workstream deliverables with guidance; maintain strong project hygiene
- Support client meetings through preparation, documentation, and follow-ups
- Identify blockers early and escalate risks with appropriate context

In 6 months
- Independently manage assigned workstreams and deliverables within enterprise implementations
- Troubleshoot common data and configuration issues with minimal support
- Lead tactical client communications and present clear project updates
- Proactively manage dependencies, timelines, and risks across teams
- Lead data validation efforts and advise customers on how ingestion and configuration decisions affect their analytics

In 12 months
- Own significant portions of implementations end-to-end with minimal oversight
- Act as a trusted day-to-day partner for enterprise customers, linking work to outcomes
- Anticipate and mitigate data, project, and cross-functional risks before impact
- Contribute to process improvements, mentor peers, and demonstrate readiness for EIM role

source_jobs.title: Implementation Manager
source_jobs.description:
Arcadia is dedicated to happier, healthier days for all. We transform diverse data into a unified fabric for health. Our platform delivers actionable insights for our customers to advance care and research, drive strategic growth, and achieve financial success. For more information, visit arcadia.io.
 
Why This Role Is Important To Arcadia
 
At Arcadia.io, an Implementation Manager (IM) is a highly capable project leader who does what it takes to successfully deliver our product and is the one person responsible for bringing the customer live. This role is the single nexus of a customer getting value out of Arcadia and Arcadia getting value out of their customer. The IM provides the first interaction and touchpoint throughout the relationship. As a result the IM makes a meaningful contribution to retention and the customer experience. 
 
Implementation Managers have complete responsibility for the end-to-end design, execution, and management of implementation activities. IMs commit to running large, complex implementation projects at high quality and speed to accelerate the path to value for our customers.
 
The ideal candidate is highly organized, can manage competing priorities, understand data architecture and can communicate effectively with technical functions, client, business and clinical teams.
 
What Success Looks Like
In 3 months
- Learn about our platform, industry, and internal processes; complete onboarding requirements and Arcadia Core Platform Certification Engage with leading customers, participating in the development and execution of complex implementation plans and supporting client discussions and working sessions around implementation initiatives
- Manage components of the implementation such as integration workstreams
 
In 6 months
- Get to know the ins and outs of our technical implementation process; demonstrate knowledge of client data and platform configuration
- Independently manage multiple components of the implementation, including data integration and module implementations
 
In 12 months
- Independently manage an implementation from contract signature to go live, including all aspects of developing an implementation plan, managing project status, handling risks and issues, and coordinating across teams to deliver a successful implementation of our platform
- Obtain advanced certification on at least 1 additional module, demonstrating your knowledge of industry context, product functionality, and implementation best practices
- Contribute to implementation process improvement initiatives, defining best practices and improving tools and documentation

source_jobs.title: Lead Analyst
source_jobs.description:
Arcadia is dedicated to happier, healthier days for all. We believe that there is a better healthcare world – one powered by data. Our platform transforms complex, diverse data into a unified foundation for health, helping organizations deliver better care, boost revenue, and lower costs.
 
We’re a team of fiercely driven individuals committed to making healthcare more sustainable—and we’re looking for passionate people to help us get there.
 
For more information, visit arcadia.io.
 
 
Why This Role Is Important to Arcadia  

Love data, and want to make a difference?

We are seeking individuals who rely on data analysis to make decisions and are passionate about improving the way healthcare is delivered to millions of patients across the country.

Join the team of a high growth Population Health and Analytics software company. You will be reporting to the Director of Data Intelligence, a team of talented data and subject matter experts. At Arcadia you will have the opportunity to become an expert in Arcadia’s platform and use those expertise to support our customers with complex business decisions involving their data.

 
What Success Looks Like
In 3 months
  • Gain familiarity with Arcadia’s data model, data ingestion process as well as Arcadia’s core risk adjustment, medical economics and quality calculations
In 6 months
  • Be able to understand the entire arcadia data pipeline understanding how the different pieces operate together
  • Lead complex discussions with Arcadia customers to guide them through decision making and how to optimize their use of the product
  • Be regularly contributing to Arcadia’s core IP improving our validation process as well as partnering with our engineering team to improve how our product operates
In 12 months
  • Be an internal expert in Arcadia’s product and able to regularly support internal and external teams to maximize their decisions around data
  • Be an expert in Arcadia’s claims and clinical feeds understanding the end to end process and regularly being able to explain complex decisions to our customers

source_jobs.title: Lead Analyst, Analytics
source_jobs.description:
Arcadia is dedicated to happier, healthier days for all. We believe that there is a better healthcare world – one powered by data. Our platform transforms complex, diverse data into a unified foundation for health, helping organizations deliver better care, boost revenue, and lower costs.
 
We’re a team of fiercely driven individuals committed to making healthcare more sustainable—and we’re looking for passionate people to help us get there.
 
For more information, visit arcadia.io.
 
 
Why This Role Is Important to Arcadia

The Lead Analyst is a strategic leader within Arcadia’s Customer Insights organization, driving the most complex and high-value analytic engagements across the enterprise. This role combines technical excellence with thought leadership, influencing analytic strategy and product direction.

You will shape how Arcadia and its customers leverage data to improve population health outcomes, reduce costs, and enable smarter healthcare decisions.

 
What Success Looks Like
In 3 months
  • Establish yourself as a subject matter expert within value based analytics concepts such as Medical Economics, Risk, and Attribution
  • Learn Arcadia's data models and tech stack
  • Understand end to end pipeline from ingestion to customer facing deliverables
In 6 months
  • Drive the development of advanced analytic frameworks and reusable assets across production analytic portfolios
  • Contribute thought leadership to Arcadia’s analytics strategy and product evolution
  • Be owner of at least one Analytics Engine (risk, medecon, episodes, etc.) while contributing to others
In 12 months
  • Serve as a company-wide expert on healthcare analytics and an architect of analytics engines
  • Have a deep understanding of how our customers use our analytics products
  • Build out roadmap with product of path to make analytics engines market leading

source_jobs.title: Lead Analytics Engineer - Data Modeling & Quality
source_jobs.description:
Arcadia is dedicated to happier, healthier days for all. We believe that there is a better healthcare world – one powered by data. Our platform transforms complex, diverse data into a unified foundation for health, helping organizations deliver better care, boost revenue, and lower costs. 
 
We’re a team of fiercely driven individuals committed to making healthcare more sustainable—and we’re looking for passionate people to help us get there.
 
For more information, visit arcadia.io.
 
 
Why This Role Is Important to Arcadia 
Arcadia's data platform powers population health analytics for health plans, ACOs, and provider groups across the country. As a Lead Analytics Engineer — Data Modeling & Quality, you sit at the intersection of data quality ownership and analytical data modeling. You'll own the SQL and DBT layer that transforms raw clinical and claims data into trusted, production-grade datasets, while also serving as the quality authority for the data those models produce.

This is a hybrid role — deeper SQL and DBT expertise than a traditional Data Health Professional, with a more analytical and model-focused scope than a Data Engineering role. You're less focused on pipeline infrastructure and more on the logic, shape, and trustworthiness of the data itself.
 
What Success Looks Like
In 3 months
Independently triage and resolve pipeline data quality issues
Author at least one new DBT model or refactor an existing one to meet current modeling standards
Design a DBT test suite for a set of models lacking coverage
Understand the end-to-end pipeline from ingress through silver and gold, and be able to trace a data quality issue to its root layer
In 6 months

What Success Looks Like
In 3 months

source_jobs.title: Principal Product Manager, AI Product
source_jobs.description:
Arcadia is dedicated to happier, healthier days for all. We believe that there is a better healthcare world – one powered by data. Our platform transforms complex, diverse data into a unified foundation for health, helping organizations deliver better care, boost revenue, and lower costs. 
 
We’re a team of fiercely driven individuals committed to making healthcare more sustainable—and we’re looking for passionate people to help us get there.
 
For more information, visit arcadia.io.
 
Why This Role Is Important to Arcadia
Healthcare is entering a new era where AI can fundamentally transform how organizations understand populations, improve outcomes, reduce administrative burden, and deliver better experiences for patients and providers. Arcadia is uniquely positioned at the intersection of healthcare data, analytics, and workflow intelligence to bring these capabilities to market. 
We are looking for a Principal Product Manager, AI Products to help define and deliver the next generation of AI-powered healthcare solutions. This leader will own critical product investments that leverage generative AI, advanced analytics, and intelligent automation to solve meaningful customer problems and create measurable business value. 
Principal Product Managers at Arcadia are builders, operators, and end-to-end owners. They combine deep customer understanding, strong technical fluency, and relentless execution to bring innovative products from concept to customer adoption. They operate with significant autonomy, influence company strategy through product outcomes, and are accountable for delivering enterprise-grade capabilities that customers trust and depend on. 
This role is ideal for product leaders who thrive in fast-moving environments, can translate emerging AI technologies into real-world customer value, and are energized by shipping products rather than managing process. 
Success is measured by products delivered, customer outcomes achieved, business impact created, and the ability to establish yourself as a trusted expert with customers and cross-functional partners. 
 
What Success Looks Like
In 3 months
Develop working expertise in Arcadia’s platform architecture, customer workflows, AI strategy, and data ecosystem
Identify and prioritize high-impact product opportunities within assigned domain ownership
Ship at least one meaningful customer-facing enhancement or production capability
Demonstrate the ability to independently navigate ambiguity and drive product decisions to execution
In 6 months

source_jobs.title: Principal Software Engineer, User Management
source_jobs.description:
Arcadia is dedicated to happier, healthier days for all. We believe that there is a better healthcare world – one powered by data. Our platform transforms complex, diverse data into a unified foundation for health, helping organizations deliver better care, boost revenue, and lower costs. 
 
We’re a team of fiercely driven individuals committed to making healthcare more sustainable—and we’re looking for passionate people to help us get there.
 
For more information, visit arcadia.io.
 
Why This Role Is Important to Arcadia
Arcadia’s mission is to improve healthcare outcomes through better data and smarter technology. A critical pillar of this mission is the User Management system, which governs authentication, authorization, and user provisioning across all Arcadia’s applications.
We’re looking for a Principal Software Engineer to take technical ownership of this area—leading the rearchitecture and implementation of a robust, scalable, and secure access control framework. By the time you join, the team will have completed key architectural planning decisions; your focus will be on translating that architecture into reality.
Beyond the core platform work, this role will drive adoption of AI-augmented engineering practices across the team—bringing modern agentic development approaches that raise the standard for how we build software.
 
What Success Looks Like
In 3 months
Gain deep familiarity with Arcadia's existing User Management system — Auth0 tenants, Chimera authorization model, Hydra session management, and event-driven provisioning workflows
Complete design handoff for the new access and grants architecture, collaborating closely with product and platform teams to understand business and security requirements
Begin staged implementation of the new system, focusing on core backend services and foundational APIs
In 6 months
Lead the implementation of a centralized access control framework that replaces legacy patterns across the platform
Drive integration of new user and access models into key Arcadia applications with zero-downtime migration for internal and external users
In 12 months
Own and operate a fully modernized User Management system that delivers secure, reliable, and high-performance authentication and authorization at scale
Define and begin executing on a unified access control strategy across services — establishing the roadmap to eliminate duplicated auth logic and reduce integration surface area
Mentor engineers and guide teams across Arcadia in secure, distributed, and scalable system design — including adoption of AI-augmented development practices

source_jobs.title: Vice President of Sales, Payor
source_jobs.description:
Arcadia is dedicated to happier, healthier days for all. We believe that there is a better healthcare world – one powered by data. Our platform transforms complex, diverse data into a unified foundation for health, helping organizations deliver better care, boost revenue, and lower costs. 

We’re a team of fiercely driven individuals committed to making healthcare more sustainable—and we’re looking for passionate people to help us get there.

For more information, visit arcadia.io
 
Why This Role is Important to Arcadia 
 
Reporting to the SVP, Growth, the Vice President of Sales, Payor is charged with generating new business opportunities for Arcadia.The VP of Sales, Payor is responsible for identifying, engaging, and closing new accounts in the Payor Healthcare market, which Arcadia defines as including Commercial and Private Payors on a regional and national scale. 
 
What Success Looks Like
In 3 months
Learn all about Arcadia and what makes us special
Our sales and contracting process
Bond with your teammates and stakeholders
Play a key role in conferences
In 6 months
You will have established a solid relationship of trust and collaboration with your VPs of Sales 
You will be leading the contracting phase with prospective clients
Close your first deal
You will be very conversant in our solution and be able to run your own presentations
In 12 months

source_jobs.title: Vice President of Sales, Provider
source_jobs.description:
Arcadia is dedicated to happier, healthier days for all. We believe that there is a better healthcare world – one powered by data. Our platform transforms complex, diverse data into a unified foundation for health, helping organizations deliver better care, boost revenue, and lower costs. 
 
We’re a team of fiercely driven individuals committed to making healthcare more sustainable—and we’re looking for passionate people to help us get there.
 
For more information, visit arcadia.io.
 
Why This Role Is Important to Arcadia
 
Reporting to the Chief Commercial Officer, the Sales VP is responsible for identifying, engaging, and closing new accounts in the Provider market, which Arcadia defines as including Provider Groups, Health Systems, IDNs, Clinically Integrated Networks, and Accountable Care Organizations.
 
What Success Looks Like
In 3 months
Learn all about Arcadia and what makes us special
Acclimate to our sales and contracting process
Understand the features and functions of our solutions
Bond with your teammates and stakeholders
Play a key role in conferences
 
In 6 months
Establish a solid relationship of trust and collaboration with colleagues
Lead the contracting phase with prospective clients
Close your first deal
Become conversant in our solution and be able to run your own presentations
 
In 12 months
Full mastery of our solution
Will have closed multiple deals

notes: Healthcare data platform with a mix of technical and non-technical roles
